subject: Your account is locked!
body: Dear customer, we noticed suspicious activity. Please login at http://paypa1-update.com to verify your identity immediately or your funds will be frozen forever.
tips: Check for 'Look-alike' domains (paypa1 vs paypal). Note the aggressive, threatening tone designed to make you panic.

subject: Updated Employee Handbook 2026
body: Please review the new workplace conduct policy attached. All employees must sign the acknowledgement form by Friday to remain compliant with Q1 regulations.
tips: Verify the domain suffix. Many companies use .com or .co.uk; attackers often use .net or .org for internal-sounding spoofs.

subject: Critical Patch Required: CVE-2026-1042
body: Our scans indicate your workstation is missing a critical security update. Please click 'Run Remote Patch' to authorize the IT department to deploy the fix.
tips: This is 'Subdomain Trickery.' The real domain is 'security-fix.io', not 'microsoft.com'. Real IT departments rarely ask you to click a link to 'patch' your own PC.

subject: You have won £5,000,000.00!!
body: You are the lucky winner of the mid-week draw! To claim your prize, please reply with your bank details and home address so we can send the check.
tips: If you didn't buy a ticket, you didn't win. Legitimate lotteries never ask for bank details via email to 'claim' a prize.

subject: Action Required: Failed Delivery for Order #882-192
body: The courier attempted to deliver your package but no one was home. A $2.00 redelivery fee is required. Click here to pay and schedule a new time.
tips: Smishing and Phishing often use 'Small Gains/Small Losses' (like a $2 fee) to lower your guard. Check the official Amazon app directly instead of clicking the link.

subject: Quick Task - Are you available?
body: I'm tied up in a board meeting and need you to handle something sensitive for me. Can you process a wire transfer for a vendor? I'll send the details once you confirm.
tips: This is 'Business Email Compromise' (BEC). It mimics a high-level executive. Always verify financial requests via a different communication channel, like a phone call.

subject: Unusual Login Detected
body: Someone just logged into your account from Moscow, Russia. If this wasn't you, click the 'Secure Account' button below to change your password.
tips: Check the sender's address. 'face-book' with a hyphen is a common fake. Hover over the button to see if the link actually goes to facebook.com.

subject: Overdue Invoice: INV-99021
body: Your payment for last month's cloud services is 5 days overdue. Please see the attached .zip file for the invoice and payment instructions.
tips: Be extremely wary of .zip or .html attachments. These often contain malware or credential-harvesting forms disguised as documents.

subject: You have 3 new private messages
body: Recruiters from 'Global Tech' and 'Prime Solutions' are trying to reach you regarding a job opportunity. Login to view your inbox.
tips: The .biz TLD (Top Level Domain) is rarely used by major platforms like LinkedIn (.com). This leverages 'FOMO' (Fear Of Missing Out) to make you click.

subject: Mailbox Full: 48 New Messages Pending
body: Your storage limit has been reached. You will not receive new emails until you upgrade your storage. Click 'Add 10GB' for free.
tips: The .xyz extension is a cheap domain often used by scammers. Microsoft will never use a .xyz domain for official system alerts.

subject: Your membership is on hold
body: We're having some trouble with your current billing information. We'll try again, but in the meantime, you may want to update your payment method.
tips: Generic greetings ('Hi Customer' or no name at all) are a red flag. Real services usually have your name on file for billing issues.

subject: You have (1) unclaimed Latte!
body: Congratulations! As a valued customer, we have a gift for you. Click here to claim your free drink voucher before it expires in 2 hours.
tips: Notice the missing 's' in 'Starbucks' and the use of a .net domain. This 'Time-Limited' offer is a tactic to force a quick, unthinking click.

subject: Payment Declined: Update your profile
body: We were unable to process your last payment. To keep your subscription active and avoid service interruption, please update your credit card details immediately.
tips: Subscription services are common targets. Instead of clicking the link, always go directly to the official website by typing the address into your browser.

subject: Review your Q1 Bonus Statement
body: The Q1 bonus distributions have been finalized. Please log in to the employee portal to view your statement and confirm your banking details for the deposit.
tips: This is highly effective 'Spear Phishing.' The .io domain is a red flag for internal corporate mail. Hover over the link to see if it actually leads to your company's HR system.

subject: Virus Detected on your PC
body: Windows Defender has found 3 high-risk threats on your system. Click 'Clean Now' to download our security tool and remove the infection.
tips: Real security software (like Windows Defender) will never email you to tell you it found a virus. It uses system notifications, and it never asks you to download a 'cleaner' via a link.

subject: New Meeting: Interview with Sarah Jones
body: You have been invited to a new meeting. Please click 'View Invite' to see the agenda and the video conference link.
tips: Attackers use common business tools like Calendly or Zoom to hide malicious links. If you aren't expecting a meeting with this person, don't click.

subject: Security Alert: New device logged into your Drive
body: A device 'Linux/Chrome' accessed your Google Drive files. If this wasn't you, please review your activity and secure your files here.
tips: This uses a 'Homograph' look-alike. Look closely at the domain: 'g00gle' uses zeros instead of the letter 'o'. This is a very common 'Hard' level trick.

subject: IRS Tax Refund Update
body: Our records show you are owed a refund of $1,450.22. To receive your payment via direct deposit, please fill out the form at the link below.
tips: Government agencies like the IRS or HMRC do not initiate contact via email to discuss refunds or personal financial information.

subject: Your Creative Cloud Invoice is ready
body: Your monthly invoice for Adobe Creative Cloud has been generated. View the attached PDF for a breakdown of your charges.
tips: Be cautious of unexpected invoices. Malicious PDFs can contain scripts that install malware. Check the official Adobe account page instead of opening attachments.

subject: Confidential Project - Request
body: I'm in a meeting and can't take calls. I need you to purchase 5x £100 Apple gift cards for a client and send me the codes. I'll reimburse you by end of day.
tips: The 'Gift Card Scam' relies on the victim wanting to help a boss. If a request involves buying gift cards or crypto, it is almost certainly a scam.

subject: Copyright Violation - Your account will be deleted
body: A post on your account has violated our copyright terms. You have 24 hours to appeal this decision or your account will be permanently removed.
tips: Fear is the main motivator here. Instagram uses the .com domain, not .me. They also don't typically delete accounts within 24 hours without multiple warnings.

subject: Ticket #8821: Password Reset Requested
body: A password reset was requested for your corporate account. If you did not make this request, click here to cancel it and secure your account.
tips: This is a clever trick. By clicking 'Cancel,' you are actually being led to a fake login page where the attacker steals your current password.

subject: Signature Required: Termination of Lease
body: Please review and sign the document regarding your office space lease agreement. This document requires your digital signature by EOD.
tips: The domain looks like DocuSign, but it's actually 'work-portal.com'. Attackers use complex subdomains to hide the real destination of the link.

subject: URGENT: Your lottery payout is waiting
body: Dear Winner, your email address was selected for a $10,000,000 payout. Please provide your passport copy to begin the transfer.
tips: Asking for identity documents like passports is a sign of 'Identity Theft' phishing. Never send sensitive documents to unverified email addresses.

subject: Package Delivery Problem - Address Update
body: We tried to deliver your parcel but the label was damaged. Please click here to verify your address so we can complete the delivery.
tips: Commonly known as 'Smishing' (when sent via text) or Phishing. It relies on the fact that most people are usually expecting a package at some point.

subject: Draft Proposal for Review
body: Hey, I've been working on the draft for the new project. Can you take a look at the OneDrive link and let me know your thoughts?
tips: This is 'Contact Spoofing.' Because it uses a personal-looking address (outlook.com) and friendly language, it feels safe. Always verify the link before clicking.

subject: Your iCloud storage is almost full
body: You have reached 99% of your 5GB limit. Photos and backups will stop working. Click here to get 50GB for free for one year.
tips: Apple uses the 'icloud.com' or 'apple.com' domains. The .xyz extension is a major red flag for phishing campaigns.

subject: Mandatory Diversity Training - 2026
body: All staff must complete the mandatory diversity and inclusion training module. Click the link to log in and start the 30-minute course.
tips: The .info domain is rarely used for internal company communications. Attackers use 'Mandatory' tasks to bypass your critical thinking.

subject: Suspicious Login Alert: New York, NY
body: A login was detected from an unrecognized device in New York. If this was not you, please use our secure portal to lock your card and change your PIN.
tips: This is a 'Visual Deception' attack. The domain is 'bancofamerica' (missing the 'k' in Bank). At a glance, it looks perfectly legitimate.

subject: Your subscription has EXPIRED!
body: Your antivirus protection has ended. Your PC is now at risk of 2,431 threats. Renew now for 90% off to stay protected.
tips: Scareware tactics use high numbers of 'threats' and aggressive formatting to make you act before you check the sender's authenticity.

subject: Your Friday morning trip with Uber
body: Thanks for riding! Your total was $45.20. If you didn't take this trip, click 'Dispute Charge' to report unauthorized activity.
tips: This uses 'Negative Incentive.' By claiming you spent money you didn't, the attacker hopes you will click the 'Dispute' button in a panic.
